summary: Kenneth demonstrates his weekly covered call selling strategy across a diversified portfolio, generating $1,400-$1,700 in premium income over the week of 9/8-9/12. He explains implied volatility (IV) as the key driver of premium income, using IV percentages to justify higher returns on volatile stocks versus blue chips, and emphasizes accepting opportunity cost rather than chasing missed gains.
takeaways: Implied volatility directly determines premium size: QBTS and D-Wave at 88% IV generate much higher premiums than Coca-Cola at 12% IV
Target high-IV stocks (70%+ IV range) for selling covered calls to maximize weekly premium income
Accept that you won't capture every gain: focus on consistent weekly premium, not hindsight-maximized returns
Roll calls early when underlying jumps significantly (Open: $5→$10) rather than wait for expiration, even if early assignment costs opportunity gains
Weekly covered call strategy on volatile micro-cap/growth stocks can generate 3-5% weekly returns versus typical 1% industry average
Patience with losing positions pays off: hold underwater positions and sell calls to generate income while waiting for recovery
Diversify across multiple positions to smooth income and reduce single-stock risk in high-volatility portfolio

strategy_candidates.change: Screen for IV percentile ≥70 when selecting new positions for the covered call wheel to maximize premium capture relative to risk
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly contrasts 88% IV (QBTS, D-Wave) generating $150+ premiums versus 12% IV (Coca-Cola) as the mechanism for outsized returns; IV is the primary lever for premium scaling on Brian's $50k desk

title: Part 15 9/8-9/12 and implied volatility explained
